headline: AI economics moved to the front of the window as pricing pressure and capex math drew the most attention.
body: Cost and pricing dominated the month: TechCrunch framed upcoming IPOs as a driver of token price increases, and a charts-based explainer broke down AI capex against hypothetical returns [0][1]. Underneath, event detection led the rotation while cost measurement, training-free methods, and LLM entered as new techniques, and quantization broke out among tools alongside fresh entrants in content addressing, lattice heap, categorical types, and MLIR. Interface direction also surfaced as OpenAI continued work on a super app with one employee declaring chat as a dominant interface is ending [5], while engineers voiced career anxiety over LLM-assisted development [10]. A separate Hacker News thread probed how tunable thinking efforts map to inference-time compute and prompt caching in Claude and ChatGPT [11].

cited_items.title: Is this the dawn of the Tokenpocalypse?
Billions spent and hypothetical returns: the AI boom explained with six charts
OpenAI is still working on that ‘super app’
LLMs are eroding my software engineering career and I don't know what to do
Ask HN: How are thinking efforts implemented?
